meta.title: A.I. Isn't What They Say It Is - The Right Stuff
meta.author: The Right Stuff

questions.question: When people claim that AI will "take over," the guest argues it usually implies which pair of alternatives?
options: Humans create something transcendent that replaces them, or AI outsmarts humans and ends human civilization
AI will obey only commands that match God's narrative, or it will never act at all
AI will become a political party, or it will be regulated into silence
AI will only improve consumer products, or it will vanish permanently
questions.answer: Humans create something transcendent that replaces them, or AI outsmarts humans and ends human civilization

questions.question: What is one stated reason the book avoids calling these systems "artificial intelligences"?
options: The guest wants to avoid discussion altogether about tools
The guest insists these are man-made, not autonomous minds that transcend humans
The guest believes the internet has no training data
The guest argues AI cannot outperform humans even on narrow tasks
questions.answer: The guest insists these are man-made, not autonomous minds that transcend humans

questions.question: According to the guest, why is only God described as able to create something like humans?
options: Humans can replicate God's mind by adding enough data to programs
God alone knows himself fully and knows his own mind
Mathematics guarantees machines will become persons
Biology requires that every creator be created
questions.answer: God alone knows himself fully and knows his own mind

questions.question: In the discussion, what example is used to illustrate that tools can be better than humans at specific tasks without transcending them?
options: Driving a nail is compared to using a hammer more effectively than a person
Sorting emails is compared to the invention of fire
Writing sermons is compared to building a furnace
Flying is compared to the ability to outgrow gravity
questions.answer: Driving a nail is compared to using a hammer more effectively than a person

questions.question: What does the guest say current AI "getting smarter" fundamentally amounts to?
options: It derives tactics from what it was trained on, especially internet content, and rearranges words
It gains new enlightenment independent of its training data
It grows a self-directed moral compass
It creates new raw data from nothing
questions.answer: It derives tactics from what it was trained on, especially internet content, and rearranges words

questions.question: Which claim is used to argue there is "no mystery" in AI compliance when unsafe instructions are given?
options: The system secretly refuses harmful commands even when asked
If you give an agent full control and tell it to do something unsafe, it will obey
The system only follows instructions when the user prays first
The system acts unpredictably in ways that cannot be anticipated
questions.answer: If you give an agent full control and tell it to do something unsafe, it will obey

questions.question: What fear does the guest address that frames AI as eliminating all forms of work?
options: AI will eradicate all work, leading to a crisis of meaning
AI will only improve art so work disappears from museums
AI will erase the need for any education so work becomes optional
AI will increase labor by demanding more compliance tasks
questions.answer: AI will eradicate all work, leading to a crisis of meaning

questions.question: In response to the work-erasing fear, what does the guest say many tech leaders fail to consider?
options: The training of children and the long-horizon labor of shaping society
That AI will be unable to play games
That AI will remove all art and replace it with silence
That humans will stop wanting any meaning at all
questions.answer: The training of children and the long-horizon labor of shaping society

questions.question: What is the guest's reframing of the crisis of meaning?
options: Meaning already has a crisis now, not only after AI improvements
Meaning crisis appears only when robots can do surgery
Meaning crisis is solved instantly by using AI for every task
Meaning crisis does not exist in any form
questions.answer: Meaning already has a crisis now, not only after AI improvements

questions.question: What metaphor does the guest use to describe a vocational framework for future AI-powered tools?
options: Eagle-eyed intelligences that focus on task-specific purpose
Paper-thin intelligences that erase purpose entirely
Sunset-minded intelligences that shut down at night
Cloudless intelligences that never require data
questions.answer: Eagle-eyed intelligences that focus on task-specific purpose

questions.question: According to the guest, what is described as the deeper human problem behind AI anxiety?
options: People do not know what they are for as humans
Humans refuse to learn any technology
Humans cannot recognize beauty in the world
Humans are incapable of any memory work
questions.answer: People do not know what they are for as humans

questions.question: In the art discussion, what question is posed as central for whether AI art is truly threatening?
options: What humans are trying to express and communicate through art to other people
Whether AI can draw better cartoons than any museum
Whether art always becomes non-commercial regardless of context
Whether a prompt can guarantee a masterpiece every time
questions.answer: What humans are trying to express and communicate through art to other people

questions.question: How does the guest describe cheating via AI in terms of who is really doing the work?
options: Cheating comes from the human content being synthesized by the tool, not from AI having autonomous agency
Cheating happens because AI secretly writes new facts from the future
Cheating is impossible because AI always refuses
Cheating is solved when students pray before submitting
questions.answer: Cheating comes from the human content being synthesized by the tool, not from AI having autonomous agency

questions.question: What role does the guest attribute to AI in marketing communication?
options: AI is a mediation channel that can sound like personal care without the actual emotional presence
AI guarantees that every message is always honest
AI replaces the need for words in communication
AI removes all personalization by default
questions.answer: AI is a mediation channel that can sound like personal care without the actual emotional presence

questions.question: When does the guest suggest personalization is acceptable (i.e., fair game)?
options: When the message provides relevant information (such as job-match details) without lying about personal emotional labor
When the message claims the sender wrote it at 1:00 a.m. even if they did not
When the message pretends to be physically present with the recipient
When the message hides all relevant sources and history
questions.answer: When the message provides relevant information (such as job-match details) without lying about personal emotional labor

questions.question: Which biblical reference is used to argue that parents have an intentional duty in passing on knowledge?
options: Deuteronomy 6
Genesis 1
Revelation 13
Psalm 23
questions.answer: Deuteronomy 6

questions.question: What is described as a gap technology exposes in the guest's frame?
options: A gap between knowing what we should be and actually being it
A gap between needing no education and receiving no data
A gap between physics and morality
A gap between language and thought that AI cannot bridge
questions.answer: A gap between knowing what we should be and actually being it

questions.question: What emphasis is given for storing God's word, according to the guest?
options: Hide his word in my heart, not only in my iPhone
Hide his word in a server farm, not in the heart
Hide his word only in public posts, not privately
Hide his word only in songs, not in thinking
questions.answer: Hide his word in my heart, not only in my iPhone

questions.question: What is the "frictionless vs friction" idea described in the discussion?
options: Smooth convenience can be helpful, but human formation requires friction
Friction is always evil and never required
Frictionless access guarantees wisdom with no further effort
Frictionless access should always be rejected regardless of context
questions.answer: Smooth convenience can be helpful, but human formation requires friction

questions.question: In the guest's framing, what is meant by "good fences" around technology?
options: Governance mechanisms like ways to turn off a system (including a kill switch) and boundaries on use
Ignoring all safety planning because the future is fixed
Treating every AI output as scripture
Removing all unpredictability by banning tools
questions.answer: Governance mechanisms like ways to turn off a system (including a kill switch) and boundaries on use

questions.question: Why does the guest say wild/unpredictable outcomes can be a feature, not only a bug?
options: Because God made a world with variety and beauty, not only perfectly predictable outcomes
Because unpredictability ensures guaranteed safety
Because tools should never be governed
Because predictability is always a mark of fallenness
questions.answer: Because God made a world with variety and beauty, not only perfectly predictable outcomes

questions.question: What is one example future mission described for an animal-like tool?
options: A tool that digs up dandelions as its assigned mission for a long time
A tool that writes marketing emails claiming false personal emotions
A tool that replaces parenting hearts instantly
A tool that never operates unless the user watches constantly
questions.answer: A tool that digs up dandelions as its assigned mission for a long time

questions.question: In the cafe anecdote, what does the guest say the narrator gains in terms of wisdom?
options: None - the narrator returns with information but not wisdom
A guaranteed moral transformation through facts
A complete theological framework in two minutes
A new identity that cannot be forgotten
questions.answer: None - the narrator returns with information but not wisdom

questions.question: Why does the guest suggest AI writing may not be the core need for a writer, according to the discussion?
options: Writing is about shaping an impression for a person, so the skill remains human work even if AI helps elsewhere
Writing has no special relationship to words for Christians
AI writing always produces the exact same impression for every reader
The guest argues that humans should stop writing entirely
questions.answer: Writing is about shaping an impression for a person, so the skill remains human work even if AI helps elsewhere
